item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I liked it while it was subtle… but as I reached the 'screenie' part, it was too on the nose.
Neutral vote, the idea itself is quite good.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hah, of course they switch hospitals if there's a lot of inactivity! If the mobs stop respawning, naturally you'll queue for a different instance! :D

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Reading the title I thought it was going to be about anomalous surgeons who perform surgery with their words.

Well, good enough. Especially that last log got me hard.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
(Upon manifesting, SCP-2169-2-25 began vocalizing loudly, grabbed a scalpel, and began stabbing the subject repeatedly in the chest.)
SCP-2169-2-25: TRO-LO-LO-LO-LO-LO-LO-LO-LO-LO-LO

Glad to know that interdimensional anomalous beings can also be assholes.

EDIT: I just had a realization. They are playing a video game! When they play the game, it takes place in our dimension! Mind totally blown! +1!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
SCP-2169-2-25: TRO-LO-LO-LO-LO-LO-LO-LO-LO-LO-LO

Upvoted in spite of this line, but this is absolutely horrificly off key.

item.title: STAFF POST - OPEN
item.content:encoded: In cases of minor errors, it's totally fine to just fix it. The weirdly defensive explanation isn't necessary.

item.title: Tiny Grammatical Error
item.content:encoded:
I noticed a tiny grammar error,
SCP-2169 is an anomalous phenomenon that affect a single operating room with an adjoining observation suite, designated SCP-2169-1. It should be affects. I'm not trying to be a grammar nazi about it, but i know some troll will, and down vote this solely based on that.
I'm only trying to prevent that from happening.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Upvoted just for the TROLOLO line.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I got it when the guy said he'd become unemployed so he'd be on more often, because boy have I had that conversation before.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm going to have to second this question.

"Whatever happened to the person wheeling the gurney into the room?" just kept repeating in my head the moment that I read the room becomes inaccessible. It broke my suspension of disbelief into little pieces, and than stomped around on the little pieces until they were ground into dust.

Possible Fix? Hmmm, let's see.
What if the phenomenon can activate if a -solitary- person wheels the gurney into the room alone? Make them either freeze in place like a statue or just plain vanish and pop back into existence once the SCP is finished. Have them be able to only partially / cryptically explain what they experience.

How about if the gurney pusher re-manifested absolutely convinced they should be receiving a monetization cut from Youtube, but confused and hazy, unable to put a finger on exactly *why* and no such video featuring the person would ever be found on the internet?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You basically just turned Surgeon Simulator into a SCP.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Holy crap I figured it out by the time the word "screenie" was uttered. Love that twist.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Eerie, yet hilarious.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
TRO-LO-LO-LO-LO-LO-LO-LO-LO-LO-LO

Everything was going well until this.
You didn't need to do that. There was no good reason for it.

E:
To be a little more specific, such things always seem to be a grab for upvotes based on 'oh hey i recognize this fleeting internet thing lol' rather than the actual merit of an article. I think this was good enough on its own without needing to resort to that.

item.title: Re: dated
item.content:encoded:
"Outdated" doesn't equal "meaningless". It's the 21st century and we can still comprehend the idea of slang from previous ages (if I remember correctly, we get the word "dude" from Yankee Doodle). The quote would remain as originally stated in Foundation documentation anyway; it's not like we edit old newspapers nowadays into "modern language" when we read through stuff in archives.

In the end it's your opinion and your vote. These are just my thoughts in response.

item.title: Re: dated
item.content:encoded:
I wouldn't use slang that will become meaningless in a few years.

It's been around for about 5 years, and its been ingrained in Internet culture so much I don't see it dying off anytime soon.

I would have to disagree with you, in saying that it breaks the 4th wall. I'm not sure if it's only me, but I see the Foundation's universe as Our universe + Foundation. As in, it's generally the same, maybe with a few slight alterations. That's probably why it doesn't surprise me to see some sort of Internet stuff like that to be in a few anomalies; why wouldn't they be influenced by humans?

item.title: dated
item.content:encoded: I don't like when a story dates itself using stuff like "TRO-LO-LO-LO-LO-LO-LO-LO-LO-LO-LO". I wouldn't use slang that will become meaningless in a few years. It just breaks the 4th wall for me.

This was… Surprisingly good.

Scratch that, it was very good.

Although it implies that superdimensional beings from the furthest reaches of existence can be unemployed, very bored or simply trolls. That made the article for me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You turned Surgeon Simulator 2013 into an SCP.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
When the patient scheduled for surgery within the affected operating room is sedated and moved into the room, the affected operating room will become unreachable

Subsequently, individuals designated SCP-2169-2 will manifest within the room

What happens to the nurse/attendant/person who moved the patient into the room? Do they become trapped, or does the effect only activate when the patient is alone in the room?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Any similarities are coincidence, I actually didn't even remember that one until you brought it up.

item.title: +1 Why do I have so many feels
item.content:encoded:
…that fourth log.

SCP-2169-2-11: We're done?

SCP-2169-2-1: Yep! See, that wasn't so bad.

SCP-2169-2-2: Yay!

SCP-2169-2-11: Aww, thanks guys. I really appreciate it.

SCP-2169-2-2: You should totally take a screenie.

…this is pretty much what happened the first time I led a dungeon run in an MMORPG I was beta-testing. I was… level 40 or something, and a group of newer players (levels in the 20s) was basically running around in front of the dungeon entrance looking for someone with long-distance attacks (I played an archer class). We cleared the dungeon and managed to all get some good loot, and then the newbie group took a bunch of screenies. Yay screenies.

I still have those screenshots. Guh. I miss that game. Take your +1, Aelanna, while I go reminisce and stare at my old weapons and gear.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Never got into MMO games, but I still liked this one.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I feel like this is what would happen if Surgeon Simulator became an MMO… and was slightly more accurate. Jokes aside, this was so much fun to read. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I have no idea what's going on here and I love it,. Good job Aelanna.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'll clarify. -1 is the operating room; the observation suite is reachable while it is active, but you can't get into the room to interfere with the surgery itself.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I thought I knew what was going on, and then I had no idea what was going on.

What the hell? I love it, though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like it. Does it have anything to do with Zoroaster's Well, the other one that implied reality was a videogame?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
When the patient scheduled for surgery within the affected operating room is sedated and moved into the room, the affected room will become unreachable, with any attempt to open the doors failing. Subsequently, individuals designated SCP-2169-2 will manifest within the room and begin to perform the scheduled surgical procedure on the subject:

During the procedure, instances of SCP-2169-2 will converse among themselves on a number of esoteric and often anomalous topics. When the procedure is complete, all instances of SCP-2169-2 will demanifest and SCP-2169-1 will become reachable, after which the affected patient can be removed.

I guess SCP-2169-1 is an observation suite, not an operating room, right?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: A specific hospital. I'll clarify that, thanks.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yep, fixing.

And yes, it's meant to be a parody of player behavior in MMORPGs.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The fact that this was a coldpost done in 1 hour and 11 minutes makes it so much better. Heartily upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
SCP-2169 is an anomalous phenomenon that affects operating rooms with adjoining observation suites, designated SCP-2169-1.

Does this effect every hospital which falls under this criteria? Or did you forget to mention a specific hospital? Bit confused there.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Aww, this was so cute!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
SCP-2169-2-1 — Appears to be a middle-aged male of indeterminate ancestry, and has filled the role of the surgeon in 83% of recorded activations. Has repeatedly been called "Dave" by other instances of SCP-2169-1.

I think you mean SCP-2169-2, not SCP-2169-1.

Other than that I think I like this. It is supposed to be a game right?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm going to have surgery today and this made me uncomfortable Thank you Aelanna.

+1'd
